why: Natural query/selected emission order cannot be used directly: retained 32x512 has 23.813x ideal reduction but about 1.000x natural-order run reduction.
The retained 32x512 segment distribution is strong enough for segmented reduction: p50 segment length is 23, p90 is 32, and 94.94% of selected pairs are in segments with at least 16 rows.
GPU route prepack by (tile_id, compressed_group) is cheap enough as a diagnostic contract: about 0.57 ms median for 1.57M valid pairs.

Previous hash-tile and K16 row-leader kernels failed because they used local atomics or reduced too small a route scope; the next branch should consume the sorted segment metadata and avoid per-contribution local atomicAdd.
implementation_contract: Build sorted valid pair metadata: pair_pos_sorted, segment_keys, segment_starts for token_block=32 and selected_block=512.
One reducer unit owns one tile-group segment and D16 or D32 slice.
Compute selected-row contributions with MFMA-shaped H64 dot reuse, then reduce rows for the owned segment before one global dKV write.
Keep this as materialized-probs diagnostic first; fuse probability, LI, and compressor recompute only after the segment reducer beats the sparse atomic gate.

promotion_gate.first_target: materialized-probs segmented reducer plus pack below 8.8 ms at S4096 layer2

commands: docker run --rm --entrypoint python --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --ipc=host --network=host --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -e HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 -e PYTHONPATH=/workspace/tools/dsv4_runtime_probe:/workspace/sources/wip/torchtitan -v /scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527:/workspace -w /workspace onenexus/nexus-titan:rocm722-pytorch-nightly-mori tools/dsv4_runtime_probe/analyze_sparse_mla_shared_kv_actual_li_tile_segments.py --seq-len 4096 --layer-id 2 --token-blocks 16,32,64 --selected-blocks 128,256,512 --summary-path /workspace/run_artifacts/actual_li_tile_segment_lengths_s4096_layer2_tb16-64_sb128-512_20260603.json
